title: What Is Retrieval Augmented Generation
text: Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) combines document retrieval with text generation. Instead of relying only on model memory, the system fetches relevant documents at query time. This often improves factual grounding and reduces hallucinations in enterprise assistants.

title: How Embeddings Support Semantic Search
text: Embeddings transform text into dense vectors where semantic similarity is represented as geometric closeness. In a vector database, nearest-neighbor search retrieves chunks related by meaning rather than exact keyword overlap.

title: Why Reranking Matters in Retrieval Pipelines
text: Initial retrieval is fast but noisy. A reranker evaluates a query-document pair with a deeper model to improve ordering. Strong reranking can significantly increase answer relevance, especially for ambiguous or long queries.

title: Resume Tips for AI Engineers
text: A strong AI engineer resume emphasizes deployed systems, measurable outcomes, and ownership. Recruiters look for impact metrics such as latency reduction, quality improvements, or cost savings. Mention production tooling, observability, and testing practices.

title: Startup Execution and Product Velocity
text: Early-stage startups benefit from short feedback cycles. Teams should ship measurable increments, collect customer feedback quickly, and prioritize learning speed over perfection. Product velocity requires clear goals and lightweight processes.

title: Hybrid Search: Combining BM25 and Vectors
text: Hybrid retrieval blends lexical and semantic signals. BM25 excels when exact terms matter, while embeddings catch paraphrases. Weighted score fusion often outperforms either method alone for broad knowledge bases.

title: Building Feedback Loops for LLM Apps
text: Feedback loops convert user reactions into ranking signals. Positive votes can up-weight successful chunks while negative votes reduce exposure for weak evidence. Over time, the assistant adapts retrieval behavior to user needs.

title: Explainability in AI Assistants
text: Explainable assistants disclose why an answer was produced. Common techniques include listing retrieved passages, showing confidence or relevance scores, and citing sources. This improves trust and helps debug retrieval failures.
